legal_context: Rights of Irina Oleinic / Relation to CASE-MACHERET-1997-2026

subjects.affected_party: Irina Oleinic (Spouse of A©tor / Мачерет А.А., ИДНП 2000001159655)

transaction.description: Household transfer from A©tor to spouse Irina Oleinic
transaction.classification_by_court: Alleged receipt of funds from UN/IMF origin

accusation_logic.state_position: Funds classified as international origin (UN/IMF); percentage withheld on grounds that recipient lacks international protection status
accusation_logic.legal_consequence: Irina Oleinic stripped of A©tor's protective status; declared 'foreign agent' (inoagent)

incident_facts.procedural_violation: Application of punitive measures against third party (spouse) as collateral pressure mechanism against primary subject

legal_qualification.primary: Art. 189 CP RM — Extortion via familial leverage
legal_qualification.secondary: Art. 327 CP RM — Abuse of official position
legal_qualification.echr: Art. 8 ECHR — Right to respect for private and family life
legal_qualification.jus_cogens_nexus: Escalation of institutional hostility following proven blockade (Node_Absolute_Blockade); collateral targeting of family constitutes aggravated violation of erga omnes obligations

causal_chain_position.pattern: Institutional hostility expanding beyond primary target to family members

conclusion: This node records the expansion of institutional hostility beyond the primary target to family members. The accusation that a 3000 MDL household transfer constitutes receipt of UN/IMF funds, and the consequent deprivation of Irina Oleinic's protected status, is consistent with the established pattern of procedural sabotage documented across 8158 days. This constitutes collateral pressure in violation of Art. 8 ECHR and erga omnes obligations under Jus Cogens.
